output_summary.refresh_scope_binding: Public-safe synthetic fixture only with explicit exclusions.
output_summary.metadata_delta_inventory: Workflow rename, projection update, surface ref assessment, owner-decision preservation, and optional lineage event candidate.
output_summary.rag_surface_inventory: Manifest, cards, triage, review, decision, owner decision, metadata index, retrieval trace/evaluation, and graph lens surfaces are refresh candidates.
output_summary.rag_manifest_refresh_packet: Refresh metadata pointers only and mark stale/blocked refs by reference.
output_summary.source_slice_card_refresh_set: Refresh source-slice cards by pointer state only.
output_summary.source_slice_triage_refresh_packet: Inventory delta refs, flag stale refs, preserve owner-decision linkage, no source payload access.
output_summary.source_slice_review_refresh_packet: Refresh queue pointers and keep approval state unchanged.
output_summary.source_slice_decision_packet_refresh: Retain non-approved owner-decision references without converting to approval.
output_summary.owner_decision_record_refresh_candidate: Carry owner-decision refs forward as references only.
output_summary.metadata_index_refresh_packet: Sync delta pointers and preserve provenance linkage.
output_summary.retrieval_trace_evaluation_refresh: Refresh trace pointers and exclude embedding/vector/BM25 payloads.
output_summary.graph_lens_export_candidate: Refresh graph export pointers only; no ontology or canon promotion.
output_summary.boundary_review_note: Metadata refresh only; no source text, answer payload, Drive mutation, vector artifact, approval, or authority claim.
output_summary.gaps: Concrete stale ref identifiers and real execution are not present in the fixture.
output_summary.downstream_handoff: Apply pointer-only updates only after authorized concrete refs are available; optional metadata-only lineage event.
output_summary.no_claims: No source truth, answer authority, owner approval, ontology/canon promotion, default route switch, repo/private payload access, or command execution claim.
